2605.28170.title: Localizing Input Uncertainty Quantification for Large Language Models via Shapley Values
2605.28170.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into high-stakes decision-making, the ability to reliably quantify uncertainty has become a critical requirement for safety and trust. However, current uncertainty quantification methods primarily operate at the output level, often failing to distinguish whether uncertainty arises from the model's lack of knowledge or from ambiguity in the user's input. While input-centric uncertainty quantification has recently emerged as a promising direction, it remains relatively underexplored and typically relies on coarse, input-level information. Consequently, users are provided with scalar uncertainty scores that offer little actionable guidance on which parts of the input should be clarified to improve reliability. To address this limitation, we propose Shapley-based input uncertainty Quantification (ShaQ), a framework for span-level attribution of input-induced uncertainty. Our approach models ambiguous spans in the input as players in a cooperative game and quantifies their contributions using Shapley values, defined via the weighted average of marginal reductions in conditional entropy obtained by clarifying each span coalition. Unlike existing input-level approaches, our formulation captures complex interactions among spans and provides a principled decomposition in which individual attributions sum exactly to the total input-induced uncertainty. We evaluate ShaQ on the AmbigQA and AmbiEnt benchmarks, where it achieves state-of-the-art performance in ambiguity detection. We further demonstrate its utility on MediTOD, showing that ShaQ can localize under-specified clinical utterances and facilitate human-AI collaboration in high-stakes settings. Overall, ShaQ improves uncertainty estimation and provides actionable insights for targeted input clarification.
2605.28170.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地融入高風險決策中，可靠量化不確定性的能力已成為安全性和信任的關鍵需求。<lb/>然而，目前的不確定性量化方法主要在輸出層面運作，往往無法區分不確定性是來自模型知識的缺乏還是來自用戶輸入中的模糊性。<lb/>雖然以輸入為中心的不確定性量化最近出現為一個有前景的方向，但它仍然相對未被充分探索，並且通常依賴於粗略的輸入級信息。<lb/>因此，用戶獲得的標量不確定性分數對於哪些部分的輸入應該澄清以提高可靠性提供的可行指導有限。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們提出了基於Shapley的輸入不確定性量化（ShaQ），這是一個針對輸入引起的不確定性進行跨度級歸因的框架。<lb/>我們的方法將輸入中的模糊跨度建模為合作遊戲中的參與者，並使用Shapley值量化它們的貢獻，這些值是通過澄清每個跨度聯盟所獲得的條件熵邊際減少的加權平均來定義的。<lb/>與現有的輸入級方法不同，我們的公式捕捉了跨度之間的複雜互動，並提供了一個原則性分解，其中個別歸因的總和恰好等於總的輸入引起的不確定性。<lb/>我們在AmbigQA和AmbiEnt基準上評估了ShaQ，在模糊性檢測中達到了最先進的性能。<lb/>我們進一步展示了它在MediTOD上的實用性，顯示ShaQ可以定位不明確的臨床表述，並促進高風險環境中的人機協作。<lb/>總體而言，ShaQ改善了不確定性估計，並為針對性的輸入澄清提供了可行的見解。
2605.28170.author: Seongjun Lee et.al.
2605.28170.authors: Seongjun Lee, Suwan Yoon, Changhee Lee
